physical.weight_g.basis: None of the three sources document the empty holder weight. S2 (Keystone) links to a category page, not a specific product drawing with weight data. physical.length_mm.basis: The model code '503450' encodes 5.0mm thick × 34mm wide × 50mm long per industry LiPo naming convention. S1 (Adafruit 503450 product page) explicitly uses this model code and lists a 50mm length cell. S3 (SparkFun LiPo guide) also references 503450-format cells confirming the naming convention. Two independent vendor sources corroborate via the standardized model code.

physical.width_mm.basis: 503450 model code encodes 34mm width; S1 and S3 both reference this form-factor designation, confirming the 34mm dimension via the industry-standard LiPo cell naming convention.

physical.weight_g.basis: S1 (Adafruit product page for the 1000mAh 503450 LiPo) is the only class source that specifies a weight value for this specific cell. S2 (Battery University) and S3 (SparkFun) discuss LiPo chemistry/usage but do not list the weight of a 503450 1000mAh cell specifically.

electrical.voltage_nominal_v.basis: 3.7V nominal is the universally established voltage for 1S lithium-polymer chemistry. S1 (Adafruit product listing), S2 (Battery University), and S3 (SparkFun) all independently confirm 3.7V nominal for 1S LiPo cells. Three independent credible sources agree.

physical.length_mm.basis: S1 (USB 2.0 spec) covers electrical protocol, not module PCB dimensions. physical.width_mm.basis: Same reasoning as physical.length_mm — no class source documents the 30mm width of this generic USB 5V module. The value is a representative estimate.

physical.height_mm.basis: No class source specifies the 18mm height for this generic USB 5V power module. This represents a typical populated PCB + USB-A connector height but is not drawn from any of the listed sources.

physical.weight_g.basis: No class source documents the weight of this generic USB 5V module PCB. The 15g estimate is plausible for a small PCB with USB-A socket and terminal but cannot be verified from S1, S2, or S3.

electrical.voltage_output_v.basis: USB 2.0 specification (S1, official usb.org) mandates VBUS at 5V ± 5%. S2 (Adafruit USB Breakout guide) and S3 (SparkFun USB Power guide) both confirm 5V output from USB-A. Three independent sources — one being the official USB-IF specification — corroborate the 5.0V output voltage.
